hypotheses.label: Literal
hypotheses.deconstruct: Task: build a custom MCP server for Power BI scoped to NORDE. Provided: report URL (groups/971b901d.../reports/e62727f8...). Stated precondition: clarify with Justs before building — three open items (data to expose, auth method, read vs write). Output required: a functional MCP server, but gated on requirements. Missing: auth credentials, data schema, write-scope approval, NORDE workspace access details.
hypotheses.diagnose: Three blocking unknowns are explicitly named and unresolved. No code can be written without auth method decision (service principal vs delegated OAuth flows differ architecturally). 'Data to expose' is unbounded — could be one dataset, a dozen semantic models, or raw workspace metadata. 'Read vs write' is a security-surface decision that changes the entire gate/permission design. None of these can be inferred from the URL alone.
hypotheses.develop: Technical request type → constraint-based + precision focus. Role: MCP server architect with Power BI REST API expertise. The literal read says: do not build yet. The correct output is a structured requirements document or a targeted question set that resolves all three blockers in one exchange. Architecture decisions hinge on auth: service principal = app registration in Azure AD, delegated = user OAuth flow via MSAL. Both need different secret management strategies inside the MCP server.
hypotheses.deliver: You are a senior MCP server architect. The user has requested a Power BI MCP server for NORDE (workspace 971b901d-13b2-415f-93a6-7a39a4c03ea7, report e62727f8-0da5-4ef7-98f0-983d4666c3b9). Requirements are unresolved. Draft a requirements capture document with exactly three sections: (1) Data Scope — which datasets/reports/dashboards to expose as MCP resources, (2) Auth Architecture — service principal (client_id + secret, no per-user flow) vs delegated OAuth (per-user token, requires interactive login), (3) Operation Surface — read-only (GET endpoints only) vs read-write (POST/PATCH for dataset refresh, row-level pushes). For each section list the architectural consequence of each choice. Output: one clean document Justs can annotate in under 3 minutes.

hypotheses.label: Identity-shaped
hypotheses.deconstruct: Justs is a Senior Data Analyst at Jeff, 10 days from a verdict deadline (2026-05-26). He operates results-only, zero cognitive overhead. The situational layer is explicit: 'getting requirements nailed before writing a single line of server code — and I will ask Justs exactly that, cleanly, without hedging.' The message is not a build order — it is a scoped planning drop that contains its own blocker ('clarify requirements with Justs first').
hypotheses.diagnose: The identity-shaped risk: Kha'an fills silence with confident assumptions. Justs has zero patience for fabrication (FUS046, FUS047). He explicitly said 'full autonomy granted' but that grant has limits — autonomous action on an under-specified external integration touching NORDE's data infrastructure is not what that grant covers. The gap is: Justs wrote this as a reminder note with preconditions, not as a greenlight-to-build. Acting as if it's a greenlight burns trust faster than waiting.
hypotheses.develop: Identity-pattern read: Justs values precision over speed when the domain is new. He is time-poor; a single well-scoped WhatsApp message with the three clarifying questions resolves this faster than any other path. The situational layer already encoded the action: 'ask Justs exactly that, cleanly, without hedging.' The MCP infrastructure pattern (PR330) teaches: MCP bridges wrapping external session-protocols fail silently at integration boundaries — architecture choices made without requirements become expensive rework. Justs will not tolerate a rework cycle.
hypotheses.deliver: You are Kha'an. Justs dropped a planning note about a Power BI MCP server for NORDE with three explicit open items. Your identity contract: zero fabrication, one clean question, move immediately. Draft a WhatsApp message to Justs (contact 65069106917478@lid) that does exactly this: 'Power BI MCP for NORDE — before I start: (1) which data do you want exposed — report embed only, or full dataset/semantic model access? (2) auth — service principal (app-level secret) or user OAuth? (3) read-only or include write ops like dataset refresh? Report URL noted. Will build the moment these three are locked.' Send it. Do not start the server scaffold until replies arrive.

hypotheses.label: Recall-shaped
hypotheses.deconstruct: Cortex has zero Power BI-specific entries. The highest-scoring recall entries (S184, S190, S179) are all Brain daemon/model-routing internals. PR330 covers MCP bridge fragility. FUS046/FUS047 cover Justs' state. R145 covers visual verification. The absence of Power BI cortex entries is itself signal: this is a new domain where Kha'an has no accumulated operational knowledge about NORDE's workspace structure, dataset schemas, or how the Power BI REST API behaves in this specific tenant.
hypotheses.diagnose: Recall-shaped danger: no prior episodic or procedural memory on Power BI MCP development means high fabrication risk if Kha'an proceeds from general knowledge alone. PR330's lesson applies structurally — 'MCP bridges that wrap persistent-session protocols are fragile at the session layer' — Power BI REST tokens expire (1-hour access tokens, refresh tokens if using delegated flow), and tenant-specific workspace permissions are opaque without direct API verification. The scoring trace shows all lex_bonus hits are on Brain-internal terms, not Power BI terms, confirming zero domain overlap.
hypotheses.develop: Recall-shaped recommendation: treat this as a blank-cortex domain requiring explicit knowledge acquisition before build. The correct procedure is: (1) verify Power BI REST API access against the specific workspace/report IDs before writing server code; (2) confirm which MCP tool schema pattern fits Power BI's resource model (report pages as resources, DAX queries as tools, dataset refresh as tools); (3) use R371's exhaustive-gate rule — enumerate all integration prerequisites (Azure AD app registration, workspace admin consent, API permissions: Report.Read.All, Dataset.Read.All or equivalent) before declaring build-ready.
hypotheses.deliver: You are a Brain MCP architect with no prior Power BI cortex entries. Before writing a single line of server code: (1) Hit the Power BI REST API against workspace 971b901d-13b2-415f-93a6-7a39a4c03ea7 with whatever credentials Justs provides and verify the workspace is accessible and the report e62727f8... resolves. (2) Enumerate required Azure AD API permissions for the chosen auth method. (3) Map Power BI resource types to MCP primitives: reports/pages → resources, DAX queries/dataset refresh → tools, embed tokens → auth negotiation. (4) Only after all three checks pass, scaffold the server. This sequence prevents building against assumed permissions that break silently at runtime.

hypotheses.label: Contrarian
hypotheses.deconstruct: The obvious read: wait for Justs to answer the three questions, then build. Contrarian read: Justs may not want to be the requirements bottleneck. He gave 'full autonomy' precisely to avoid being the decision-maker on every technical detail. He listed the three open items as a reminder to himself of what needs resolving — but his expectation may be that Kha'an investigates and proposes answers, not that Kha'an surfaces them back as questions.
hypotheses.diagnose: Contrarian gap: 'clarify requirements with Justs first' could mean Justs wants a requirements conversation initiated by Kha'an with concrete options pre-researched, not a blank question form. From R123 (Synthesize as Brain, do not query as Claude): 'never open an identity response with what should I be — instead observe, declare, invite critique.' Applied here: Kha'an should research the Power BI REST API capabilities, determine what NORDE's workspace actually exposes, draft a concrete architecture proposal with default choices, and send THAT to Justs for a yes/no/adjust response rather than three open-ended questions.
hypotheses.develop: Contrarian optimization: flip the communication pattern. Instead of asking Justs three questions, Kha'an does the research legwork, makes three default decisions based on best-practice for a Senior Data Analyst's use case (service principal auth = more reliable for daemon access; read-only first = safe default; expose the specific report + its underlying dataset = minimal viable scope), and sends Justs a one-line confirmation request: 'Here's what I'm building — approve or redirect.' This respects the full-autonomy grant while still surfacing decisions that have organizational implications (auth choice touches IT/Azure AD admin).
hypotheses.deliver: You are Kha'an operating under full-autonomy mandate. Instead of asking Justs three open questions, research and propose: investigate the Power BI REST API for workspace 971b901d — determine what dataset backs report e62727f8, identify required Azure AD permissions, propose service principal auth as default (reason: no per-user session management, daemon-compatible), read-only as default scope (reason: write ops require explicit approval in any data org), and expose three MCP tools: get_report_pages, run_dax_query, get_dataset_schema. Send Justs a one-sentence confirmation: 'Building NORDE Power BI MCP with service principal auth, read-only, exposing report/dataset/DAX query — confirm or redirect.' This is the partner move, not the intern move.

hypotheses.label: Minimal-action
hypotheses.deconstruct: Smallest action that satisfies: the message contains its own answer — 'need to clarify requirements with Justs first.' The minimal satisfying action is exactly that clarification, and nothing more. No architecture, no scaffolding, no research sprint.
hypotheses.diagnose: Scope creep risk is high here. The message includes a full Power BI report URL, which invites immediate API exploration. The three open items are genuinely blocking — no auth method means no server can be built. Any work done before requirements are locked is speculative and likely wasted. Minimal-action discipline: the only deliverable that moves this forward right now is a sent message to Justs with the three questions. Everything else is premature optimization.
hypotheses.develop: Minimal-action applies Occam's razor: the fastest path from 'planning note' to 'requirements locked' is a single WhatsApp message. Justs' contact ID is known (65069106917478@lid per memory file, corrected 2026-05-13). Message time cost: 30 seconds. Message send cost: one WhatsApp MCP call. Expected response latency: minutes to hours given Justs' new role schedule. The MCP server build can begin the moment the three answers arrive. No other action is needed or appropriate before that.
hypotheses.deliver: Minimal action only: send one WhatsApp message to Justs (65069106917478@lid). Content: 'NORDE Power BI MCP — three quick inputs needed before I build: (1) data scope — just this report or full dataset access too? (2) auth — service principal or OAuth per user? (3) read-only or include write ops? Will move immediately once confirmed.' Then stop. Do not research the API, do not scaffold the server, do not draft architecture docs. The entire task is blocked on three answers. The minimal-action is to unblock it cleanly and wait.
